page: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a substantial 260 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 1417 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results demonstrate strong single-core and memory capabilities. The server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2367.0 and an image processing score of 17728.0, both placing in the top tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is also top-tier at 34.92 GB/sec. Multi-threaded performance is robust, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 17385.0 and a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 171.29 GB/sec. In terms of memory bandwidth, it records 2070.94 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 166.75 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For lightweight language model tasks, the server processes 135M model prompts at 2457.26 tokens/sec and generates text at 408.05 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX24mds_v2 offers high resource density, particularly regarding its memory-to-vCPU ratio. This makes it a highly specialized option where memory capacity and high CPU clock speeds are prioritized over massive parallel core counts. However, because it lacks a dedicated GPU, it is not suited for heavy GPU-accelerated deep learning training, though its advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) provide hardware-level acceleration for CPU-based inference.

The hardware configuration and benchmark profile make the FX24mds_v2 highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads. These include large-scale in-memory databases, heavy relational database management systems, caching layers such as Redis, and enterprise applications requiring high single-core performance alongside massive memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 504.0 GB of RAM, translating to 21.0 GB per core, alongside 1417 GB of local storage. The server delivers top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2367.0 and a gzip compression speed of 34.92 GB/sec. It also exhibits strong multi-threaded capabilities with a Geekbench multi-core score of 17385.0. While it lacks a dedicated GPU, its CPU supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) for accelerated mathematical computations. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and enterprise applications requiring high single-thread performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and 1417 GB of local storage, designed for high-performance database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and 1417 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and substantial local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM and 1417 GB of local storage.
Top-tier single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2367.0.
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX for hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
